senses.definition: a small rentable plot of land that can be used for growing flowers, vegetables, etc.
a chemical or natural material that is added to the soil to improve its productivity and help plants grow

examples.x: The gardener added fertilizer to the soil to help the plants grow strong and healthy.
After applying the fertilizer, the flowers bloomed beautifully within weeks.
The soil in my garden was very poor, so I mixed in some fertilizer to improve it.
Too much fertilizer can harm plants, so it is important to follow the instructions.

senses.definition: a tool with a long wooden handle attached to a heavy steel or iron blade, primarily used for chopping wood and cutting down trees

examples.x: He used an ax to chop firewood for the winter.
The ax had a sharp blade that made cutting easier.
She learned how to handle an ax safely during the camping trip.
The lumberjack carried his ax as he worked in the forest.
He polished the wooden handle of his grandfather's old ax.

senses.definition: a big plastic bag containing fertilized soil that can be used for growing plants or fruits, such as vegetables or tomatoes

examples.x: I planted some tomatoes in a growbag because there is no space in the garden.
The growbag is easy to move around, so I can place it in the sunniest spot.
He decided to use a growbag for his herbs on the balcony.
She filled the growbag with compost to ensure the plants get enough nutrients.

senses.definition: a long and thin stick made of the hard and hollow stem of plants such as bamboo, used for keeping garden plants in place
a gardening tool with a handle and three or four sharp points, used for digging or moving hay

examples.x: The fork's tines were perfect for breaking up compacted dirt.
She enjoyed using a fork to mix compost into her garden bed.
He found the fork useful for turning over leaves in the fall.
He relied on the fork to mix soil amendments into his potting mix.
She used the fork to prepare the vegetable patch for planting.

senses.definition: an electronic piece of equipment that closely resembles a chainsaw and is capable of cutting and trimming hedges in a garden

examples.x: The electric hedge trimmer made cutting through thick branches much faster.
He used the hedge trimmer to shape the bushes in front of his house.
She borrowed her neighbor's hedge trimmer to cut back the overgrown shrubs.
After buying a new hedge trimmer, he found trimming the hedges much faster and more efficient.
He opted for a battery-powered hedge trimmer to avoid dealing with long cords.

senses.definition: a gardening tool with a thin metal blade attached to a long handle, used for cultivating and breaking up the surface of the ground, as well as removing weeds
solid waste from animals spread on a piece of land to help plants and crops grow healthier and stronger
any wild and unwanted plant that may harm the process of growth in a farm or garden
a gardening tool with metal teeth attached to a long wooden handle, primarily used for gathering dead leaves or marking a spot on the ground

a protective layer of decaying leaves or compost that is spread over or around a plant to improve the quality of the soil, stop weeds from growing, or to provide protection for the plant's base and its roots
a tool that has a long handle with a broad curved metal end, used for moving snow, soil, etc.

examples.x: He found a lightweight shovel useful for camping trips.
The shovel was perfect for digging holes for planting trees.
She always kept a shovel in the shed for yard work.
He grabbed a shovel to clear the driveway after the snowstorm.
The shovel's sharp edge made it easier to cut through tough ground.

senses.definition: a gardening machine equipped with a rotating blade that is designed to cut the grass on lawns

examples.x: The lawnmower started making a strange noise, so I had to take it to the repair shop.
After using the lawnmower, the garden looked much cleaner and more organized.
She bought a new lawnmower to help her keep her large yard in shape.
He pushed the lawnmower across the yard, carefully trimming every patch of grass.

senses.definition: a brownish substance, often found under the ground or in regions with wet climate, formed by plants dying and becoming buried, that once added to the soil, can enhance its quality and help plants grow faster

examples.x: The gardener used peat to improve the soil’s ability to hold water during dry spells.
Some plants, like blueberries, thrive with peat in the soil due to its acidity.
Farmers sometimes use peat to increase soil acidity for certain crops.
The landscaper recommended adding peat to the soil before planting the shrubs.

senses.definition: decayed leaves, plants, or other organic waste turned into a mixture that can improve the soil's quality and productivity once added to it
a type of scissors used for trimming and pruning plants, featuring a sharp blade and spring mechanism for cutting through stems and branches

examples.x: She used pruning shears to cut back the overgrown branches of the rose bush.
The gardener sharpened his pruning shears before trimming the hedges.
He found his pruning shears in the shed and began clipping the unruly vines.
He carefully selected a pair of pruning shears to trim the delicate flowers without damaging them.

senses.definition: a pesticide used to control and kill rodents, such as rats and mice, and can come in the form of baits, traps, or poisons

examples.x: The farmer used rodenticide to control the rat population in the barn.
It is important to store rodenticide out of reach of pets and children.
Many people use rodenticide in their homes to prevent mice from nesting in walls.
After noticing signs of rodents, the pest control company recommended using rodenticide.
She placed the rodenticide in the attic to keep the mice from causing damage to the insulation.

senses.definition: a protective glove worn during gardening or yard work to prevent cuts and scratches and provide a better grip while handling tools and plants

examples.x: She wore her garden gloves while planting new flowers in the backyard.
I need to buy a new pair of garden gloves because mine have worn out.
The garden gloves kept my hands safe from sharp thorns as I pruned the rose bushes.
He put on his garden gloves before pulling weeds from the flower beds.
She found a set of colorful garden gloves at the store that fit perfectly.

senses.definition: a device used to apply pesticides or other chemicals to plants or surfaces to control or eliminate pests or diseases

examples.x: She carefully filled the pesticide sprayer with a mixture of chemicals before spraying her garden.
After reading the instructions, he adjusted the nozzle on the pesticide sprayer for a fine mist.
The farmer used a pesticide sprayer to protect his crops from harmful insects.
They wore gloves and masks while using the pesticide sprayer to avoid exposure to harmful chemicals.
Before applying the pesticide sprayer, they checked the weather to ensure there would be no rain.

senses.definition: a tool used for creating defined edges along garden beds, walkways, and other landscaped areas

examples.x: After mowing the grass, she used a garden edger to tidy up the edges of the garden.
He found that a garden edger made it much easier to maintain the boundaries of his garden.
To give the yard a more polished look, I decided to buy a new garden edger.
The garden edger worked perfectly for shaping the curved lines of the flower bed.
Using a garden edger saved him time compared to doing the same work by hand with a spade.

senses.definition: a small living part of a plant that when put in the ground, grows into a new one

examples.x: The gardener carefully planted seeds in the fertile soil, eager to watch them grow into vibrant flowers.
Farmers select high-quality seeds to ensure a bountiful harvest and healthy crops.
Some plants, like dandelions, disperse their seeds through the wind, allowing them to spread far and wide.
With proper care and attention, even the tiniest seed has the potential to grow into a towering tree.

senses.definition: a tool used to remove weeds and other unwanted plants from gardens and flower beds

examples.x: The gardener used a garden weeder to remove the stubborn weeds growing between the flowers.
I spent the afternoon pulling weeds from the flower beds with my trusty garden weeder.
She grabbed the garden weeder to clear the path of unwanted plants that were crowding the garden.
After a few minutes of using the garden weeder, the garden was free of weeds and looked neat.
The garden weeder made it easy to get rid of deep-rooted weeds that were hard to pull by hand.

senses.definition: a small hand-held gardening tool used for loosening soil, removing weeds, and cultivating around plants in tight spaces

examples.x: She used the hand cultivator to loosen the soil around the plants in the flower bed.
After adding compost, he grabbed his hand cultivator to mix it into the garden soil.
He carefully worked the hand cultivator into the soil to aerate it before planting the seeds.
She used the hand cultivator to prepare the soil for the new vegetable garden.

senses.definition: a sturdy gardening tool with a four-sided metal blade attached to a long handle, which people use to move earth
a small plot of land that is used for growing a particular type of crops or plants
an object with two handles and one wheel, used for carrying things

examples.x: He filled the wheelbarrow with soil for the garden.
The construction crew used a wheelbarrow to move bricks around the site.
She pushed the wheelbarrow carefully over the uneven ground.
The wheelbarrow made it easier to transport heavy items to the backyard.
She decorated her wheelbarrow for a fun garden display.

senses.definition: a tool used to apply liquid fertilizers, herbicides, pesticides, and other solutions to plants and garden beds

examples.x: I used the garden sprayer to water all the flowers in the backyard.
She filled the garden sprayer with a mixture of water and fertilizer to help the plants grow.
He checked the garden sprayer for leaks before using it to treat the shrubs.
It took only a few minutes to cover the garden with the garden sprayer and prevent any pest damage.
After filling the garden sprayer, I adjusted the nozzle to make the spray finer.

senses.definition: a chemical substance or agent that is used to kill or control insects that can cause harm to humans, crops, animals, and structures

examples.x: He read the label carefully to make sure the insecticide was safe for his plants.
After applying the insecticide, the plants seemed healthier and free from bugs.
The house was treated with insecticide to get rid of the ants that had invaded the kitchen.

senses.definition: an electrical, handheld gardening tool for cutting grass or weed, used as an alternative for larger machines, such as lawnmower

examples.x: He used a strimmer to tidy up the edges of the lawn after mowing.
The strimmer quickly cleared the overgrown weeds from around the fence.
After trimming the hedges, he grabbed the strimmer to clean up the edges of the garden.
I borrowed a strimmer to help clear the path in the backyard.

senses.definition: a tool used for planting bulbs, such as tulips, daffodils, and other spring-flowering bulbs

examples.x: She used a bulb planter to quickly dig holes for the daffodils in her garden.
The gardener handed me a bulb planter to help with planting the tulip bulbs.
After marking the spots, he pushed the bulb planter into the soil to place each bulb at the right depth.
The children took turns using the bulb planter to plant the spring flowers in the garden.

senses.definition: the process of modifying the visible features of an area of land, such as adding plants, changing the terrain, or constructing structures, to improve its aesthetic appeal or make it more functional
a area of ground that provides the essential needs for seeds to grow and develop into young plants before they are moved to another place

examples.x: After a few weeks, the plants in the seedbed were strong enough to be moved to the field.

senses.definition: a tool that sprays a fine mist of chemicals to control pests and insects

examples.x: The gardener used a fogger to control the mosquito problem in the backyard.
After the rain, the farmer used a fogger to spray pesticides over the crops.
She filled the fogger with a special solution to get rid of the mites on her plants.
The fogger covered the entire garden, ensuring that every corner was treated.

senses.definition: a motorized gardening tool with a high-speed air nozzle used for blowing leaves and debris off lawns and other surfaces

examples.x: I used the leaf blower to clear the driveway after the storm.
The gardener brought his leaf blower to clean up the fallen leaves in the garden.
My neighbor uses a leaf blower every weekend to tidy up the yard.
It took less time to clear the lawn with the leaf blower than it would have with a rake.
After mowing the grass, he used a leaf blower to remove the clippings from the walkway.

senses.definition: a hand-held cutting and pruning tool used for gardening tasks such as digging, cutting, and pruning

examples.x: He used a garden knife to carefully trim the overgrown roots.
She grabbed her garden knife to divide the plant into smaller sections.
The garden knife was perfect for cutting through tough weeds in the flower bed.
The garden knife helped him clear the soil around the young plants without damaging them.
With a garden knife in hand, she removed the unwanted branches from the bush.

senses.definition: to make decayed leaves, plants, or other organic waste into a mixture that can improve the soil's quality to help plants grow more quickly

examples.x: She composts kitchen scraps and yard waste to create nutrient-rich soil for her garden.
They compost grass clippings and fallen leaves to produce organic fertilizer for their plants.
The farmer composts manure and crop residues to enrich the soil and increase yields.
Composting food waste reduces landfill waste while improving soil health.
Composting coffee grounds and eggshells adds valuable nutrients to the soil.

senses.definition: to cut off the top parts of a tree, bush, or other plant to either reduce their size or help them grow

examples.x: The gardener advised cutting back the rose bushes in early spring to stimulate new growth.
To maintain the hedge's neat appearance, it's essential to cut back the overgrown branches regularly.
The orchard owner decided to cut back the fruit trees to ensure a more bountiful harvest the following season.
It's advisable to cut back herbs regularly to prevent them from becoming too leggy and to encourage bushier growth.
Before winter, many gardeners choose to cut back perennial plants to protect them from frost and promote vitality in the next growing season.

senses.definition: to cut through something at its base in order to make it fall

examples.x: The lumberjacks had to cut down the old oak tree to clear space for the new construction project.
Environmentalists protested the decision to cut down a grove of ancient trees for the development of a shopping mall.
The city had to cut down several diseased trees to prevent the spread of the infection to healthier ones.
In preparation for the storm, residents were advised to cut down weakened or precarious branches from their trees.
The lumberjack skillfully cut down trees with powerful swings of his ax.

senses.definition: to remove earth or another substance using a tool, machine, or hands

examples.x: The archaeologist used a shovel to dig for ancient artifacts.
Miners use heavy machinery to dig for valuable minerals.
The treasure hunter carefully dug for buried treasure using a metal detector.

senses.definition: to cultivate and nurture plants in an outdoor space, either as a job or hobby

examples.x: She gardens in her backyard, growing vegetables and flowers for her family.
They garden together on weekends, planting herbs and shrubs in their yard.
He gardens organically, using natural methods to nurture his plants.
She gardens with passion, experimenting with different plants and techniques.

senses.definition: to cut and join a piece of a living plant to another plant so that it can continue growing from there
to cause a plant to develop and give fruit or flowers

examples.x: She grows beautiful roses in her garden.
This region is known for growing apples.
We need to grow more vegetables for the restaurant.
They're planning to grow sunflowers this summer.
He's trying to grow organic strawberries.

senses.definition: to break up the surface of the ground, remove weeds, etc. using a gardening tool with a thin metal blade attached to a long handle
to apply solid waste from animals or plants to a piece of land in order to enhance the quality of its soil and help plants grow ever more quickly
to put a seed, plant, etc. in the ground to grow

examples.x: Each spring, the community comes together to plant flowers in the town square.
It's a family tradition to plant a new tree for each child's first birthday.
The children were excited to plant pumpkin seeds and watch them grow.
To attract butterflies, you should plant some milkweed in your garden.
We plant fresh herbs in small pots to keep in the kitchen.

senses.definition: to place a plant in a container, made of plastic or clay, filled with fertilized soil

examples.x: She decided to pot the new herbs and place them on the kitchen windowsill.
The gardener spent the morning potting seedlings in the greenhouse.
He carefully potted the cactus, making sure not to hurt its roots.
They potted the flowers and arranged them on the patio for decoration.
After buying the sapling, she potted it in rich, fertilized soil.

senses.definition: to make a ground surface become level or smooth, using a special gardening tool

examples.x: He rakes the soil in the garden to prepare it for planting.
She rakes the leaves in the yard to keep it tidy.
The groundskeeper rakes the sand in the playground to remove debris.
After mowing the lawn, he rakes to collect grass clippings and create a neat appearance.
She rakes the gravel pathway to even out the surface for walking.

senses.definition: to cut grass, wheat, etc. with a gardening machine or handheld tools, such as a scythe

examples.x: Every weekend, he spends time mowing the lawn to keep it well-manicured.
The farmer used a tractor to efficiently mow the large field of wheat.
To maintain a neat appearance, the gardener mowed the wild grass around the garden.
Using a traditional scythe, the worker mowed the grass in the historic garden.
She grabbed the lawnmower to quickly mow the backyard before the gathering.

senses.definition: to cover the soil around plants with a layer of material like wood chips or leaves to retain moisture, suppress weeds, and regulate soil temperature

examples.x: She mulches her flower beds with wood chips to conserve moisture and prevent weed growth.
They mulch their vegetable garden with straw to protect the soil from erosion and maintain soil moisture.
He mulches the garden paths with gravel to prevent mud and enhance aesthetics.

senses.definition: to use a rounded blade attached to a long handle to dig or move earth

examples.x: Gardeners shovel soil into flower beds for planting.
During winter, homeowners shovel snow from driveways and sidewalks.
Construction workers shovel dirt to create foundations for buildings.
Landscapers shovel mulch around trees and plants.
They shoveled soil into flower beds.

senses.definition: to plant seeds by scattering them on the ground

examples.x: She sows tomato seeds in the garden every spring for a fresh harvest.
He sows wildflower seeds along the roadside to create a vibrant meadow.
The farmer sows wheat seeds in the field to cultivate a crop for harvest.
They sow pumpkin seeds in the backyard garden for homemade pies in the fall.
Sowing lettuce seeds in rows ensures a plentiful supply of fresh greens for salads.

senses.definition: to remove a plant from its original place and replant it somewhere else

examples.x: Gardeners often transplant young seedlings from the nursery to the garden beds for better growth and spacing.
To create a more harmonious landscape, the landscaper decided to transplant mature trees to different areas of the park.
The homeowner decided to transplant the rose bush to a sunnier spot in the backyard.
During the landscaping project, the crew carefully transplanted ornamental shrubs.
Farmers may transplant crops like rice or tomatoes to optimize spacing.

senses.definition: to pour water on the ground to make plants grow in it

examples.x: After planting the seeds, be sure to water them regularly.
Every morning, she goes out to water her vegetable garden.
She set up an automatic sprinkler system to water her flowers.
We water the herbs on the balcony with a small watering can.
While on vacation, I asked my neighbor to water my indoor plants.

senses.definition: to spread small plant parts over soil to start their growth

examples.x: She seeds the garden beds with flower seeds in the spring for a colorful display.
He seeds the lawn with grass seeds to fill in bare patches and promote healthy growth.
The farmer seeds the field with soybean seeds to cultivate a crop for harvest.
They seed the vegetable patch with cucumber seeds to yield fresh produce for the summer.
Seeding the meadow with wildflower seeds creates a natural habitat for pollinators.

senses.definition: a long pole with a cutting blade or saw at the end, used for trimming high branches or foliage

examples.x: The gardener used a pole pruner to trim the overgrown branches high up in the tree.
I need to buy a pole pruner to help me cut the branches that are too high to reach.
After the storm, we used a pole pruner to clear the broken branches from the top of the tree.
Using a pole pruner, he carefully shaped the tree, removing the extra branches.

senses.definition: a flat, rectangular blade with a sharp edge, used for cutting through roots, edging, and digging in hard or compacted soil

examples.x: The worker used a square point shovel to scoop up the gravel and move it into the wheelbarrow.
For the landscaping project, they needed a square point shovel to dig a precise trench.
He grabbed the square point shovel to level the soil before planting the garden.
She used the square point shovel to scrape the excess dirt from the edge of the foundation.
The gardener preferred a square point shovel for its sharp, flat edge when transferring compost.

senses.definition: a rounded blade, used for digging and moving loose materials like soil, gravel, and sand

examples.x: He used a round point shovel to dig a hole for the new tree.
The gardener grabbed the round point shovel to loosen the packed soil.
She relied on a round point shovel to clear the dirt from the flower beds.
The construction worker used a round point shovel to level the ground for the pathway.
After the rain, the softened soil was easy to move with a round point shovel.

senses.definition: used to refer to a plant or tree that is currently producing new foliage

examples.x: The trees in the park are in leaf, showcasing their vibrant green foliage.
